data.title: Shanghai Bio-Heart Wins NMPA Nod for Bioabsorbable Coronary Stent
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2185) ) just unveiled an update.

Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology has received approval from China’s National Medical Products Administration for its Bioheart bioabsorbable drug-eluting stent system, indicated for patients with ischemic heart disease caused by native coronary lesions to improve intracoronary lumen diameter. The self-developed scaffold is designed to provide temporary vessel support, elute an anti-proliferative drug to prevent restenosis, and then fully resorb, aligning with the clinical goal of vascular restoration and potentially enhancing the company’s position in the evolving coronary intervention devices market, though the firm cautions there is no guarantee of ultimate commercial success.

The Bioheart system functions similarly to a traditional drug-eluting stent while embodying a “leave nothing behind” philosophy by avoiding permanent implants once the vessel has healed. Publication of pivotal trial results in JACC: Cardiovascular Interventions underscores the technology’s clinical validation and may bolster confidence among clinicians and investors, but the company urges shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution as product development and market adoption remain subject to uncertainty.

More about Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. is a PRC-based medical device company focused on cardiovascular interventions, developing bioresorbable scaffolds and drug-eluting stent systems for percutaneous coronary intervention. The group targets patients with coronary artery disease and ischemic heart conditions, aiming to advance vascular restoration therapies in the Chinese market.

Average Trading Volume: 632,660

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$596.4M

data.title: Dynagreen Leaves Nearly Entire RMB2.36 Billion Convertible Bond Issue Unconverted
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1330) ) has shared an update.

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group reported that its A share convertible corporate bonds, issued in February 2022 for RMB2.36 billion with a six-year term and listed in Shanghai under the name Dynagreen Convertible Bonds, have undergone multiple conversion price adjustments following consecutive annual and interim equity distributions and an A-share incentive scheme. These price resets, which lowered the conversion price from RMB9.82 at issuance to RMB8.67 per share as of July 2026, are designed to reflect shareholder returns and employee incentives while maintaining bondholder conversion economics.

In the second quarter of 2026, only 112 A shares were converted from the Dynagreen Convertible Bonds, worth RMB1,000, bringing total converted bonds since 2022 to RMB143,000, or just 14,798 shares, representing 0.0011% of pre-conversion share capital. As of 30 June 2026, RMB2.359857 billion of the bonds, or 99.9939% of the original issue size, remained outstanding and available for conversion until February 2028, indicating limited bondholder appetite to convert so far and leaving Dynagreen with a largely intact convertible debt overhang on its balance sheet.

More about Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated environmental services company focused on waste-to-energy and related environmental protection projects, with its A shares and convertible bonds traded in mainland China and H shares listed in Hong Kong. The group finances its operations partly through onshore A-share convertible corporate bonds issued on the Shanghai Stock Exchange, reflecting its reliance on capital markets to support long-term infrastructure and environmental initiatives.

Average Trading Volume: 1,751,659

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$10.31B

data.title: Richly Field China Development Narrows Loss but Takes Heavy Impairments in 2026
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Richly Field China Development Limited ( (HK:0313) ) has issued an announcement.

Richly Field China Development Limited reported a sharp decline in revenue to HK$41.9 million for the year ended 31 March 2026, down from HK$123.2 million a year earlier, with gross profit nearly halving amid continued weakness in its property operations. The group posted a net loss of HK$214.1 million, narrower than the prior year, but results were heavily hit by substantial impairments on goodwill, other receivables and investment properties, highlighting ongoing asset quality pressures and underlining the financial strain on the developer as it navigates a difficult real estate market.

The company also recorded lower selling and administrative expenses and a modest income tax credit, but financing costs remained high and profitability was further undermined by valuation losses on its investment properties. While some reversal of expected credit losses on trade receivables provided minor relief, the scale of impairments indicates continued headwinds for stakeholders, signalling persistent balance-sheet risks and limited near-term earnings visibility for the Hong Kong-listed developer.

More about Richly Field China Development Limited

Richly Field China Development Limited is a Hong Kong-listed property developer with operations conducted through its subsidiaries. The group focuses on investment properties and related real estate activities in mainland China, deriving revenue from property sales and associated services in a challenging market environment.

Vala Inc. reported that it delivered 509 vala automobiles in the second quarter of 2026 and surpassed 1,000 cumulative deliveries of its vala home model within six months, underscoring strong early market recognition for its mobile living space concept. The company highlighted its “co-creator” owner-sales network, which grew to 775 participants, and said this community-driven model is helping to form a mobile store network that broadens brand reach among lifestyle-focused consumers.

To deepen customer engagement, Vala has opened self-operated vala underground city stores in 13 major cities, combining vehicle displays, lifestyle products and offline community activities to create a one-stop experiential ecosystem. The firm is also scaling its digital and community footprint via the valalife WeChat Mini Program, a growing vala club events platform and substantial social media traction, moves that reinforce its ambition to lead China’s emerging new-energy mobile lifestyle segment and strengthen ties with both existing and prospective car owners.

Its flagship vala automobile features a factory-installed pop-up roof, modular interior design and entertainment systems, targeting consumers seeking lifestyle-oriented, vehicle-based living and travel experiences. The company pursues an asset-light manufacturing model and multi-channel sales to build a differentiated presence in the new-energy mobility market.

Voyah Automotive Technology Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:7489) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Voyah Automotive Technology reported June 2026 deliveries of 14,223 vehicles, up 41% year-on-year, bringing year-to-date deliveries to 76,264 units, a 36% increase that underscores its momentum in China’s competitive new energy vehicle market. The company attributed part of this growth to its self-developed AI agents and large-model-based digital marketing system, which it says has lifted sales operation efficiency by about 30% and is being expanded across the full value chain.

The automaker is accelerating its all-electric strategy with the planned July launch of the Passion S, a sporty all-electric SUV aimed at younger drivers, featuring four LiDAR sensors and advanced chassis control for high-end intelligent driving. At the same time, Voyah will begin mass deliveries of the all-electric Taishan X8, a large five-seat flagship with a 120kWh Amber battery-based pack, moves that broaden its product lineup and are intended to create new growth drivers in the premium EV segment.

Great Wall Motor reported total sales of 108,080 vehicles in June 2026, down 2.36% year-on-year, while year-to-date sales rose 2.48% to 583,895 units, supported by strong growth in NEV and ORA brand volumes. Production increased 9.8% in June to 119,262 units and 9.57% year-to-date, highlighting a ramp-up in output even as some traditional models, including Haval and TANK, saw weaker monthly sales.

data.title: TransThera Completes Global Phase III Enrollment for Tinengotinib in Advanced Cholangiocarcinoma
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An update from TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc. ( (HK:2617) ) is now available.

TransThera Sciences has completed patient enrollment in a global multicenter, randomized Phase III trial of its lead drug tinengotinib as an oral monotherapy for advanced cholangiocarcinoma with FGFR gene aberrations that has relapsed or become refractory after chemotherapy and prior FGFR inhibitor treatment. The study spans centers in the U.S., Europe and Asia, and compares tinengotinib with physician’s choice therapy, positioning the company to potentially address a high unmet need in a market projected to grow from US$2.0 billion in 2024 to US$3.2 billion by 2027.

Tinengotinib is currently the first and only investigational therapy worldwide in late-stage development for CCA patients who have failed both chemotherapy and FGFR inhibitors, supported by Orphan Drug and Fast Track designations from the U.S. FDA and Orphan Drug status from the EMA for biliary tract cancers. With prior trials showing favorable safety and anti-tumor activity, the successful progression of this registrational program could significantly enhance TransThera’s position in oncology, offering a potential new late-line option for heavily pretreated patients and strengthening its standing with regulators and investors.

More about TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc.

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing), Inc. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on discovering and developing innovative small-molecule therapies for solid tumors. Its core pipeline includes tinengotinib, a multi-kinase inhibitor targeting FGFRs, VEGFRs, mitotic kinases and JAK, with ongoing trials in cholangiocarcinoma, prostate, breast and liver cancers and regulatory fast-track support in major markets.

Average Trading Volume: 5,465,893

L.K. Technology Holdings Limited ( (HK:0558) ) has issued an update.

L.K. Technology Holdings reported a 3.7% decline in revenue to HK$5.61 billion for the year ended 31 March 2026, as softer demand and higher costs compressed margins. Gross profit fell 13.9%, while gross margin slipped to 24.5%, indicating pressure on pricing and cost control.

data.title: Venus Medtech Explores Up to RMB500 Million Investment via Shares and Convertible Bonds
data.description:

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Venus Medtech (Hangzhou), Inc. Class H ( (HK:2500) ) just unveiled an update.

Venus Medtech (Hangzhou) Inc. has signed a non-binding investment term sheet with Hangzhou Hi-Tech, Hangzhou Yingzhiqin and other potential investors for a prospective capital injection of up to RMB500 million. The funding would be raised through a mix of new share subscriptions and new convertible bonds, with pricing linked to the recent average trading price of the company’s Hong Kong-listed shares and capped at HK$2.5 per share.

Under the proposal, investors would subscribe for new shares at 100% of the 20-day average closing price and for new convertible bonds at 110% of that average, both subject to the price cap, potentially providing Venus Medtech with additional growth capital and balance sheet flexibility. The parties have agreed to pay RMB100 million in earnest money within seven business days, which is refundable if the transactions fail due to the company’s fault, but the deal remains subject to definitive documentation, regulatory approvals and shareholder mandate, meaning there is no assurance it will proceed and investors are urged to act cautiously.

More about Venus Medtech (Hangzhou), Inc. Class H

Venus Medtech (Hangzhou) Inc. is a China-based medical technology company focused on developing and commercializing cardiovascular implantable devices and related interventional solutions. Listed in Hong Kong, the company targets both domestic and international markets for advanced heart valve therapies and other structural heart disease treatments.

Mirxes Holding Company Limited has provided a quarterly update on its efforts to resume trading in its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, following delays in publishing its 2025 annual results and annual report. The company has established an independent investigation committee to oversee a forensic probe into prepayments and significant cash outflows, and is working with the board to align the scope of this investigation with resumption guidance issued by the exchange.

JL MAG Rare-Earth Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6680) ) has issued an announcement.

JL MAG Rare-Earth Co., Ltd., a Chinese producer of high-performance rare-earth permanent magnets, supplies critical magnetic materials to sectors including new energy vehicles, wind power and industrial automation. The company leverages its rare-earth processing capabilities to support strategic industries that depend on stable, high-specification magnet solutions.

The group expects a strong interim performance for the first half of 2026, projecting net profit attributable to shareholders of RMB400 million to RMB460 million, up about 31% to 51% year on year. Profit after excluding non-recurring items is forecast to rise 57% to 83%, with second-quarter earnings also showing sharp gains, underscoring improved core profitability and signalling a solid operating momentum for investors despite the figures remaining unaudited.

More about JL MAG Rare-Earth Co., Ltd. Class H

JL MAG Rare-Earth Co., Ltd. is a Chinese rare-earth materials producer focused on manufacturing high-performance rare-earth permanent magnets and related products. The company serves downstream sectors such as new energy vehicles, wind power, industrial automation and consumer electronics, positioning itself as a key supplier in strategic emerging industries that rely on advanced magnetic materials.

data.title: CA Cultural Technology Group Boosts Margins but Posts Wider Annual Loss
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd. ( (HK:1566) ) has issued an update.

CA Cultural Technology Group reported revenue of HK$360.4 million for the year ended 31 March 2026, a slight 1.8% decline from the prior year, but gross profit surged 35.9% to HK$121.1 million as cost of sales fell and gross margin improved to 33.6%. The company also recorded higher other income and reduced administrative expenses, reflecting efforts to tighten operations and improve profitability at the gross level.

Despite operational improvements, the group’s loss attributable to shareholders widened sharply to HK$99.9 million, more than triple the previous year, with basic loss per share rising to 8.5 HK cents. High finance costs of over HK$106 million and an overall rise in expenses such as selling, marketing and distribution weighed heavily on the bottom line, underscoring ongoing balance sheet and funding pressures for investors and other stakeholders.

More about CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd.

CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd. operates in the cultural and technology sector, providing products and services that generate revenue primarily from sales and related services in Hong Kong and surrounding markets. The group focuses on technology-driven cultural offerings, with its performance closely tied to cost management, financing structure and overall efficiency of operations.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$60.28M

data.title: Nanjing Leads Biolabs Grants Over 2.25 Million H Share Awards to Staff Under Incentive Scheme
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest announcement is out from Nanjing Leads Biolabs Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9887) ).

Nanjing Leads Biolabs has granted a total of 2,254,691 H share awards to 149 employees under its H Share Award Scheme, including executive director and CFO Mr. Zuo, with all awards satisfied by existing shares acquired on the market to avoid dilution of current shareholders. The awards, priced at nil with a reference market price of HK$49.32 per H share, are subject to structured vesting schedules and individual performance targets tied to functional benchmarks, and are designed to incentivize new and existing staff and align their contributions with the group’s long-term operational and growth objectives.

The vesting for Mr. Zuo will occur in three tranches between September 2026 and January 2028, while other employees’ awards will vest over 36 or 48 months, sometimes with shorter initial periods due to administrative delays. The board and remuneration committee state that the flexible vesting periods remain within scheme rules and market practice, aiming to enhance employee retention and motivation without altering the company’s capital base, which may strengthen Nanjing Leads Biolabs’ competitive position in attracting and rewarding key talent in the biopharmaceutical sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:9887) stock is a Buy
with a HK$100.50 price target.

see the HK:9887 Stock Forecast page.

More about Nanjing Leads Biolabs Co., Ltd. Class H

Nanjing Leads Biolabs Co., Ltd. is a biotechnology company listed in Hong Kong, focusing on research-driven operations and the development and commercialization of biopharmaceutical products. The group relies heavily on specialized functions such as research and development, sales and marketing, and business development to support its long-term growth strategy.
